description: Admins have total control over the system's economic logic, user access, and final settlement structures.

features.title: Incentive Schemes (The Logic Engine)
features.usage:
Schemes are the mathematical DNA of the system. You can configure:
1. **Percentage**: (Sale Value * Rate). Ideal for standard sales.
2. **Tiered Based**: If (Sale >= Threshold) then (Sale * Bonus Rate) else (Sale * Base Rate). Used to incentivize high-ticket deals.
3. **Fixed Per Qty**: (Quantity * Rate). Best for volume-based commodities.
4. **Quantity Threshold**: If (Qty >= Threshold) then (Qty * Bonus Rate) else (Qty * Base Rate). Rewards multi-unit orders.
features.tips: Pro Tip: Always set 'Start Date' and 'End Date' correctly. The system uses the Sale Date to match the correct scheme version.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Never edit a scheme's base logic (e.g., changing it from % to Tiered) while users are attached. Create a NEW scheme instead.

features.title: User Approvals & Role Mapping
features.usage: New users remain in 'Pending' until an Admin approves their profile. You must assign them a Role and, if they are Salespeople, a Manager and an Active Scheme.
features.tips: Pro Tip: Use the 'Department' field to filter reports later in the Analytics dashboard.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Do not leave users without a Manager assignment; this breaks the hierarchical approval workflow.

description: Managers bridge the gap between sales and finance, ensuring accuracy and performance.

features.title: The Sales Audit Log (Logs)
features.usage:
Every sale logged by your team appears in the Audit Log. Each log has a unique 'SL-' Reference. You can:
1. **Approve**: Moves the status to 'Earned' for payout.
2. **Reject**: Sends it back with notes for correction.
3. **Flag**: Suspends the log for dispute resolution.
features.tips: Pro Tip: Filter by 'Pending Review' every morning to keep the payout pipeline moving.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Do not approve logs with missing product descriptions; finance will reject the entire batch later.

features.title: Batch Settlement Construction
features.usage: Batches are 'Containers' for payments. You select 'Earned' logs and group them into a Batch (BT- Reference). A batch moves from Draft -> Submitted -> Paid.
features.tips: Pro Tip: Give batches descriptive names like 'North-Region-Q1-Payout' for better tracking.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Do not delete a batch once it has been 'Submitted' to Finance. Use 'Return to Draft' if changes are needed.

description: The front-line users who drive revenue and track their financial growth.

features.title: Real-time Sales Logging
features.usage: Enter deal details. The system performs 'On-the-fly' calculation based on your assigned scheme. You can see your predicted commission BEFORE you hit submit.
features.tips: Pro Tip: Accurately enter the 'Sale Date'. If the date falls outside your scheme window, the system uses a 5% fallback rate.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Do not log sales for clients that haven't signed a contract. The 'Notes' field should contain invoice numbers.

features.title: Earning Transparency
features.usage: Track your 'Paid' vs 'Pending' commissions. The dashboard shows a 'Target Gauge' indicating how close you are to your next bonus threshold.
features.tips: Pro Tip: Export your personal ledger to PDF monthly for your own financial records.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Do not try to edit a sale once it has been 'Approved' by a manager. Contact them for adjustments.

description: Responsible for final verification of settlements and disbursement of funds.

features.title: Financial Batch Disbursement
features.usage: Accounts staff only see batches that are 'Submitted' or 'Approved'. Verify the bank details, process the transfer, and then update the batch status to 'Paid'.
features.tips: Pro Tip: Always cross-reference the Batch Total with your bank's 'Transaction Success' report.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Do not mark a batch as 'Paid' without attaching a bank transaction reference ID in the system notes.

description: Technical logic that ensures IncentivePro remains a professional financial tool.

features.title: Professional Reference Identifiers
features.usage:
The system uses two permanent ID types:
1. **SL-YYYYMMDD-XXXX**: Sales Log IDs. Unique for every individual deal.
2. **BT-YYYYMMDD-XXXX**: Batch IDs. Unique for every payment collection.
features.tips: Pro Tip: These IDs are searchable. You can type an SL or BT code into any search bar to find the exact record.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Never treat these IDs as optional. They are the 'DNA' of the system's audit trail.

features.title: The Commission Matrix (Schemas)
features.usage: Schemas (Incentive Schemes) define the math. You can create multiple schemas for different roles or products. A schema can be 'Fixed' (constant) or 'Dynamic' (threshold-based).
features.tips: Pro Tip: Use 'Tiered' schemas to push salespeople toward higher-value deals.
features.what_not_to_do: Restriction: Avoid creating overlapping schemas for the same user; the system will prioritize the most recently created one.
